About.title: A trusted partner with continuous operations
About.description: We have been continuously providing international and domestic freight forwarding services, organizing solution-based logistics operations.
About.introP1: Andjintrans LLC has been continuously providing international and domestic freight forwarding services since 2011, organizing solution-based logistics operations tailored to each cargo's characteristics (type, size, timeline, temperature/hazard requirements, documentation).
About.introP2: We coordinate air, sea, rail, and road transport individually or in multimodal combinations, managing import/export/transit shipments under one plan and one responsibility. Monitoring shipment progress, documentation, and timeline planning from a single point reduces delays and provides exceptional advantages for project cargo and regular supply chains.
About.missionDescription: To deliver transport-logistics solutions that get our clients' cargo safely, on time, and at optimal cost — with high accountability and transparent operations.
About.visionDescription: To become a trusted standard-setter in Mongolia's market for chemical and special-condition cargo transportation, introducing best international trade and logistics practices.
principles: We prioritize environmentally and socially responsible service.
We uphold workplace safety as our top priority.
We maintain honest and fair principles with our employees, partners, and clients.
About.partnersDescription: We work together with trusted international and domestic partner organizations.
About.dgDescription: Our company has been operating continuously since 2011, with growing expertise in classification, labeling, documentation (SDS/MSDS), packaging, and route risk management for chemical and dangerous goods (DG) transportation.

ServicesSea.title: Cost-Effective
ServicesSea.description: Sea freight is the most common solution for transporting large volumes over long distances cost-effectively.
ServicesSea.introP1: With FCL/LCL options tailored to your cargo volume. As a landlocked country, Andjintrans LLC plans sea-land multimodal routes through neighboring countries' ports, managing the entire process from port/terminal handling to documentation, transit coordination, and final delivery from a single point.
ServicesSea.introP2: We calculate schedules, transit times, and potential delay risks, follow documentation processing standards, and focus on reducing the risk of additional charges (storage/demurrage). We also offer suitable container and loading-securing solutions for oversized, project cargo, and shipments requiring special equipment.

ServicesRail.title: Stable Transit Times,
ServicesRail.description: Rail freight is the best option for transporting heavy, high-volume cargo at stable transit times and lower costs.
ServicesRail.introP1: Leveraging Mongolia's geographic advantages, Andjintrans LLC organizes import, export, and transit rail transport in container and wagon formats, planning multimodal routes combined with road/sea/air when needed.
ServicesRail.introP2: We manage terminal trans-loading, border crossing procedures, documentation, and loading-securing advice from a single point to reduce risks of delays and additional costs. We also offer wagon selection, securing solutions, and route studies tailored to the specific characteristics of project, oversized, and heavy cargo.

ServicesRoad.title: Flexible
ServicesRoad.description: Road freight is the most flexible option for cross-border and domestic delivery.
ServicesRoad.introP1: It offers the best capabilities for precise pickup and delivery scheduling, multi-point loading/unloading, and solving last-mile delivery. Andjintrans LLC plans road transport for import, export, and transit flows tailored to cargo type, size/weight, delivery point, and timeline requirements, organizing the delivery chain from warehouse to terminal to factory to client from a single point.
ServicesRoad.introP2: We also offer solutions for container drayage, bulk cargo, project and oversized/heavy cargo including route studies, permits, and escort transport when needed. By focusing on pre-shipment document checks, customs-terminal coordination, and timeline discipline, we aim to reduce risks of delays and additional costs.

ServicesTransit.title: Through Mongolia
ServicesTransit.description: Transit transport is the service of trans-shipping third-country cargo through Mongolian territory.
ServicesTransit.introP1: This is the form of transport that demands the highest level of route planning, timeline management, documentation, and border-terminal coordination. Andjintrans LLC plans transit flows using rail, road, and multimodal transport options, managing all stages from terminal trans-loading, border crossing, customs procedures, and cargo movement monitoring from a single point.
ServicesTransit.introP2: Transit transport delays typically arise from documentation errors, terminal waiting times, and border coordination delays, so we prioritize data and document pre-checks and route-schedule planning first. When needed, we also integrate additional solutions such as security, insurance, monitoring, and last-mile delivery to reduce risks.

ServicesInsurance.title: A Critical Part of
ServicesInsurance.description: Protection against financial losses caused by risks during transportation.
ServicesInsurance.whatDescription: Cargo insurance protects against financial losses caused by damage, loss, theft, accidents, natural risks, and transport vehicle incidents during shipping. International transport involves multiple transfers, terminals, and trans-shipments, making insurance a critical part of risk management.
ServicesInsurance.whyDescription: The carrier's liability compensation is limited in most cases (based on weight, regulations, contract conditions). Cargo insurance extends protection to match the actual value of goods and contract terms.

t1.title: All Risks
t1.description: Broadest coverage protecting against many types of risks (with detailed conditions).

t2.title: Named Perils
t2.description: Covers only specified risks (e.g., accidents, fire, sinking).

t3.title: ICC (A) – Broad Coverage
t3.description: Commonly understood as 'All Risks', widely used internationally.

t5.title: ICC (C) – Basic Coverage
t5.description: Most limited scope, basic protection only.

t6.title: Additional Coverage
t6.description: War Risks, Strikes/Riots/Civil Commotions (SRCC) when needed.

factors: Incoterms (when risk transfers), who is the insured party
requiredInfo: Special conditions: DG status, temperature regime, special packaging
claimSteps: Record and document damage/shortage upon receipt with photos/video
POD/receipt notes, damage report, arrange survey if needed
Gather invoice, packing list, transport documents (AWB/B/L/CMR/waybill), insurance certificate
Notify insurer within deadline and provide additional information as needed
ServicesInsurance.supportDescription: Andjintrans LLC advises on selecting the right insurance option for your cargo characteristics, route, and timeline requirements, helps prepare insurance documentation, and assists with claim documentation when needed.
ServicesInsurance.ctaDescription: We'll help you select the right insurance option for your cargo, route, and timeline.

ResourcesIncoterms.title: 11 International
ResourcesIncoterms.description: Incoterms® standardize where costs transfer, where risk transfers, and what responsibilities each party bears between buyer and seller.
ResourcesIncoterms.whatDescription: Incoterms® (International Commercial Terms) are rules developed by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC), widely used in international trade, that standardize where costs transfer, where risk transfers, and what responsibilities (transport arrangements, insurance, customs, etc.) each party bears between buyer and seller.
ResourcesIncoterms.whatHighlight: Incoterms are not about price — they define the boundaries of responsibility.

EXW.name: Ex Works
EXW.description: The seller makes the goods available at their premises; the buyer bears most responsibilities from that point.

FCA.name: Free Carrier
FCA.description: The seller handles export clearance and delivers the goods to the carrier (or buyer's representative) at the named place.

CPT.name: Carriage Paid To
CPT.description: The seller pays for main carriage, but risk transfers once goods are handed to the carrier.

CIP.name: Carriage and Insurance Paid To
CIP.description: Same as CPT, but the seller also arranges transport insurance (usually at a higher level).

DAP.name: Delivered at Place
DAP.description: The seller delivers to the named place; unloading is not included.

DPU.name: Delivered at Place Unloaded
DPU.description: The seller delivers and unloads at the named place.

DDP.name: Delivered Duty Paid
DDP.description: The seller handles import customs, taxes/duties and delivers 'duty paid'.

FAS.name: Free Alongside Ship
FAS.description: The seller delivers the goods alongside the vessel (at the port area).

FOB.name: Free On Board
FOB.description: The seller loads the goods onto the vessel.

CFR.name: Cost and Freight
CFR.description: The seller pays for freight; risk transfers after loading on board.

CIF.name: Cost, Insurance and Freight
CIF.description: Same as CFR, but the seller also arranges insurance.

r1.title: Container Transport
r1.description: For container transport through port terminals, FOB may not meet requirements in some cases, so FCA/CPT/CIP is often more suitable.

r2.title: DAP vs DPU
r2.description: The key difference is who handles unloading (DPU includes unloading).

r3.title: DDP Selection
r3.description: Import tax, customs, and documentation risks are high, so it's advisable to choose DDP only when an experienced party can fully manage it.

ResourcesContainers.title: 10 Types of
ResourcesContainers.description: Choosing the right container based on cargo dimensions, weight, loading method, temperature requirements, and DG status is crucial.
ResourcesContainers.selectionTitle: Key Factors When Choosing a Container
ResourcesContainers.selectionDescription: When selecting a container type, consider cargo dimensions (length/width/height), weight, loading method (from side/top), temperature requirements, moisture/vibration risks, and whether the cargo is DG (dangerous). The right container selection directly impacts cost, safety, and delay risks.

c1.title: Standard Container
c1.usage: General cargo, boxed goods, equipment, palletized cargo.
c1.advantage: Widest network availability, relatively affordable cost, available on most routes.

c2.title: High Cube Container (HC)
c2.usage: High-volume but relatively light cargo (e.g., packaged products, light industrial goods).
c2.advantage: Taller than standard 40', providing more capacity.

c3.title: Reefer Container
c3.usage: Food, pharmaceuticals, and some chemical products requiring temperature control.
c3.feature: Temperature control, air circulation, and in some cases humidity control.
c3.note: Pre-planning of set point, packaging, ventilation requirements, and plug-in availability is needed.

c4.title: Open Top Container
c4.usage: Tall/oversized cargo that needs to be loaded from above by crane/hoist.
c4.note: Securing (lashing) and protection from water/moisture is important.

c5.title: Open Side Container (Side Door)
c5.usage: Cargo that needs to be loaded lengthwise from the side, long parts/equipment, special loading conditions.
c5.advantage: Makes loading/unloading more flexible (availability depends on route).

c6.title: Flat Rack Container
c6.usage: Wide/long oversized machinery, equipment, project cargo (OOG).
c6.feature: No sides or roof (or foldable sides), suitable for oversized cargo.
c6.note: Securing, balancing, and protection solutions (securing/lashing) are essential.

c7.title: Platform Container
c7.usage: Very heavy, very large oversized cargo (when flat rack is not sufficient).
c7.note: Permits, route studies, and special securing are usually required.

c8.title: Tank Container
c8.usage: Liquid cargo, chemical products, food-grade liquids (depends on cargo type and requirements).
c8.note: DG requirements, tank cleaning, temperature conditions, proper filling/safety procedures are important.

c9.title: Ventilated Container
c9.usage: Certain goods requiring air exchange (e.g., certain grains, agricultural products — case dependent).
c9.note: Moisture, odor, and condensation risks need to be considered.

c10.title: Insulated Container (Thermal)
c10.usage: Cargo requiring protection from temperature fluctuations (without refrigeration system).
c10.note: Selected based on seasonal conditions and transit time.

ResourcesWagons.title: 9 Types of
ResourcesWagons.description: Selecting the right wagon based on cargo type, weight/volume, loading/unloading method, weather protection, and DG status is important.
ResourcesWagons.selectionTitle: Key Factors When Choosing a Wagon
ResourcesWagons.selectionDescription: When selecting a wagon type, consider cargo type, weight/volume, loading/unloading method (crane, forklift, conveyor, etc.), weather protection requirements, moisture/temperature conditions, and whether the cargo is DG (dangerous). The right wagon selection reduces risks of safety incidents, delays, damage, and additional costs.

w1.title: Box / Covered Wagon
w1.usage: General cargo requiring weather protection, boxed goods, equipment.
w1.advantage: Good protection from rain, snow, and dust.
w1.suitable: Spare parts, electronics, packaged products, fragile cargo (with packaging).

w2.title: Gondola / Open Wagon
w2.usage: Roofless, mostly for bulk and weather-insensitive cargo.
w2.suitable: Coal, ore, stone, some construction materials.

w3.title: Flat Wagon / Platform Wagon
w3.usage: Containers, wheeled machinery, long parts, equipment.
w3.advantage: Suitable for container transport and quick transfer loading at crane terminals.

w4.title: Tank Wagon
w4.usage: Liquids and gases, petroleum products, some chemicals.
w4.advantage: Designed for safe large-volume liquid transport.

w5.title: Hopper Wagon
w5.usage: Bulk/granular cargo — grain, cement, fertilizer, powder materials.

w6.title: Insulated / Temperature-Controlled Wagon
w6.usage: Cargo sensitive to temperature fluctuations (case dependent).
w6.advantage: Provides increased protection during seasonal conditions and long transit.

w7.title: Refrigerator Wagon
w7.usage: Food requiring cold chain, some pharmaceuticals/medical products (where available).

w8.title: Heavy-duty Transporter Wagon
w8.usage: Extremely heavy, long/oversized project cargo, large factory equipment.

w9.title: Special Wagons
w9.usage: Cars, timber, steel, coils, vehicles and other cargo with special transport needs.

ResourcesDangerousGoods.title: UN's
ResourcesDangerousGoods.description: Dangerous Goods (DG) are substances and products that may pose risks to human health, property, and the environment during transportation.
ResourcesDangerousGoods.whatIsDG: Dangerous Goods (DG) are substances and products that require special classification, packaging, labeling, and documentation because they may pose risks to human health, property, and the environment during transportation. DG cargo is classified into 9 UN classes (Class 1–9) internationally, and rules/requirements may differ for air, sea, road, and rail transport.

c1.title: Explosives
c1.description: Substances/articles with risk of explosion or powerful blast.

c2.title: Gases
c2.description: Compressed, liquefied, dissolved gases; includes flammable, non-flammable, and toxic sub-categories.

c4.title: Flammable Solids & Related Substances
c4.description: 4.1 Flammable solids, self-reactive substances | 4.2 Spontaneously combustible | 4.3 Dangerous when wet (emits flammable gas)

c5.title: Oxidizing Substances & Organic Peroxides
c5.description: 5.1 Oxidizers (promote fire) | 5.2 Organic peroxides (may be sensitive to heat/shock)

c6.title: Toxic & Infectious Substances
c6.description: 6.1 Toxic substances | 6.2 Infectious substances (require special medical/biological protocols)

c7.title: Radioactive Material
c7.description: Radiation risk, requires strict permits and controls.

c8.title: Corrosives
c8.description: Acids/alkalis and similar substances that corrode skin, metals, and materials.

c9.title: Miscellaneous Dangerous Goods
c9.description: Products that don't fit other classes but pose transport risks (e.g., lithium batteries, dry ice, certain magnetic materials).

ResourcesDangerousGoods.requiredDescription: The following minimum data is usually needed for quotes, proper classification, and correct packaging/labeling:

r1.description: International identification number for the substance/product

practicalTips: If unsure whether cargo is DG, first check the SDS/MSDS to verify UN information.
Common causes of DG cargo delays: classification errors, missing labels/markings, packaging standard violations, document information discrepancies.
Requirements may vary by transport mode, so getting advance consultation before loading is effective.

ResourcesPackaging.title: Cargo Packaging
ResourcesPackaging.description: Packaging protects cargo from risks during transport and makes loading/unloading operations safe and efficient.
ResourcesPackaging.whyDescription: Packaging protects cargo from vibration, impact, moisture, dust, and temperature changes during transport, making loading/unloading operations safe and efficient. Proper packaging significantly reduces the risk of damage, returns, insurance disputes, delays, and additional costs.
ResourcesPackaging.primaryDescription: Packaging in direct contact with the product.
ResourcesPackaging.secondaryDescription: Packaging that groups and protects primary-packaged products.
ResourcesPackaging.secondary_2_desc: Foam, paper, air pillows, cotton, etc.
ResourcesPackaging.tertiaryDescription: Transport unit packaging for shipping, warehousing, and loading/unloading.
ResourcesPackaging.tertiary_1_desc: Box mounted on a pallet base
common_1_items: Jumbo Bag / Big Bag (FIBC) – for ~500–2000kg bulk cargo
ResourcesPackaging.protective_4_desc: Thin base used when loading without pallets

ResourcesDocuments.title: Transport
ResourcesDocuments.description: List and description of documents required for international transport and customs clearance.

d2.name: UN Number, Proper Shipping Name, Class, Packing Group

d4.name: Temperature/Handling Instructions
d4.description: For cargo with special handling requirements
